title: System and method of monitoring respiration
abstract: A system and method for monitoring respiration of a user, comprising: a respiration sensing module including a sensor configured to detect a set of respiration signals of the user based upon movement resulting from the user's respiration; a supplementary sensing module comprising an accelerometer and configured to detect a set of supplemental signals from the user; an electronics subsystem comprising a power module configured to power the system and a signal processing module configured to condition the set of respiration signals and the set of supplemental signals; a housing configured to facilitate coupling of the respiration sensing module and the supplementary sensing module to the user; and a data link coupled to the electronics subsystem through the housing and configured to transmit data generated from the set of respiration signals and the set of supplemental signals, thereby facilitating monitoring of the user's respiration.
claims: 1. A system for monitoring respiration, the system comprising: a housing configured to be attached to an article of clothing worn by a user; a respiration sensor enclosed within the housing, the respiration sensor comprising a capacitive sensor that includes first and second conductive plates positioned parallel to one another and separated by a non-rigid material, the capacitive sensor configured to generate a respiration signal based on a change in capacitance responsive to forces resulting from movements of a body of the user caused by the user's breathing being applied to the housing, the capacitive sensor configured to not come into direct contact with the body of the user; an activity sensor configured to generate an activity signal associated with an activity performed by the user; and an electronic processing circuitry configured to obtain the respiration signal from the respiration sensor and the activity signal from the activity sensor, determine a respiration rate using the respiration signal, and transmit the respiration rate and activity data associated with the activity signal to a remote computing device.
